heading: Commission review
label: 40704 Commission review

children.heading: .—Request for Justification
children.label: a .—Request for Justification
children.text: On request of the Federal Maritime Commission, a controlled carrier shall file with the Commission, within 20 days of the request, a statement of justification that sufficiently details the carrier’s need and purpose for an existing or proposed rate, charge, classification, rule, or regulation and upon which the Commission may reasonably base a determination of its lawfulness.

children.heading: .—Determination
children.label: b .—Determination
children.text: Within 120 days after receipt of information requested under subsection (a), the Commission shall determine whether the rate, charge, classification, rule, or regulation may be unjust and unreasonable.

children.heading: .—Show Cause Order
children.label: c .—Show Cause Order
children.text: Whenever the Commission is of the opinion that a rate, charge, classification, rule, or regulation published or assessed by a controlled carrier may be unjust and unreasonable, the Commission shall issue an order to the controlled carrier to show cause why the rate, charge, classification, rule, or regulation should not be prohibited.

children.heading: .—Not yet effective
children.label: 1 .—Not yet effective
children.text: Pending a determination of the lawfulness of a rate, charge, classification, rule, or regulation in a proceeding under subsection (c), the Commission may suspend the rate, charge, classification, rule, or regulation at any time before its effective date.

children.heading: .—Already effective
children.label: 2 .—Already effective
children.text: If a rate, charge, classification, rule, or regulation has already become effective, the Commission, on issuance of an order to show cause, may suspend the rate, charge, classification, rule, or regulation on at least 30 days’ notice to the controlled carrier.

children.heading: .—Maximum suspension
children.label: 3 .—Maximum suspension
children.text: A period of suspension under this subsection may not exceed 180 days.

children.heading: .—Replacement During Suspension
children.label: e .—Replacement During Suspension
children.text: Whenever the Commission has suspended a rate, charge, classification, rule, or regulation under this section, the controlled carrier may publish a new rate, charge, classification, rule, or regulation to take effect immediately during the suspension in lieu of the suspended rate, charge, classification, rule, or regulation. However, the Commission may reject the new rate, charge, classification, rule, or regulation if the Commission believes it is unjust and unreasonable.
